regime.label: Soft-Landing Window
regime.sublabel: Iran Framework Deal + FOMC Hold Open Rate-Cut Path
regime.description: Markets entered a constructive window in June as two major overhangs began to clear. An Iran nuclear framework agreement (June 9) sent WTI crude from ~$91 to ~$82 — the first sustained move below $85 since Q1 2026 — which in turn pulled US CPI expectations lower and opened a credible path for a September Fed cut. The June 12 FOMC held rates at 3.50–3.75% but updated the dot plot to show a median of 1 cut in 2026, validating the September window. The S&P 500 has responded with a breakout above 7,500, the yield curve's 2s10s is approaching -20 bps (closest to positive since 2022), and the Fear & Greed Index has climbed to 62 (Greed). The principal remaining risks are: Iran deal ratification falling apart, US core services PCE staying above 3%, and a Fed Chair confirmation process that markets perceive as politically motivated.

riskMeter.label: Cautiously Optimistic
riskMeter.sublabel: Iran framework + FOMC dot plot upgrade have shifted the balance; rate-cut path is narrow but real. Stay selective — valuation remains stretched at 21× forward.

spreads.label: 3M–10Y
spreads.note: Still inverted but narrowing from -140 bps in March; oil-driven CPI improvement and rate-cut pricing are pulling short-end yields lower faster than long end.

spreads.label: 2Y–10Y
spreads.note: Approaching -20 bps territory — the least-inverted reading since 2022. Un-inversion is the single most important macro signal to watch in H2 2026.

spreads.label: 5Y–30Y
spreads.note: Positive long-end slope intact; term premium still being demanded by bond market given deficit concerns.

yieldCurve.inversionStatus: 3M–10Y inverted for ~23 months; 2s10s narrowing sharply — full un-inversion possible by September if FOMC cuts
yieldCurve.recessionProbability: ~23% (NY Fed 12-month model, down from 28% last month)
yieldCurve.trajectory: Oil-driven disinflation is pulling front-end yields down faster than the long end — the curve is dis-inverting constructively. A confirmed September Fed cut would likely complete un-inversion and historically mark the beginning of a strong 12-month equity period.
yieldCurve.canadianCurve: BoC curve is positively sloped (rate at 2.25% vs. 10Y GoC ~3.55%); Canadian May CPI printed 2.1% — the cleanest in the G7 right now. BoC cut in July or September is a live base-case, which is a significant tailwind for ZAG.TO and Canadian bank NIMs.

buffettIndicator.description: US total equity market cap at ~185% of nominal GDP — S&P breakout above 7,500 pushed ratio higher. AI-driven earnings premium is the main justification, but the ratio remains well above any historical definition of fair value.
buffettIndicator.implication: Quality bias and position-size discipline remain essential. The Buffett Indicator is not a timing tool, but at 185% it argues strongly against adding speculative positions at current prices.

signals.label: S&P 500
signals.note: Broke above 7,500 for the first time post-Iran shock; driven by AI mega-cap earnings and the rate-cut dot-plot upgrade. Russell 2000 also starting to participate — a broadening signal.

signals.label: TSX Composite
signals.note: TSX outperforming EM YTD — Canadian banks re-rating on BoC cut anticipation and improving credit conditions. Energy holding up despite WTI retreat as Canadian differentials tighten.

signals.label: VIX
signals.note: Back near pre-conflict lows; options market is not pricing significant near-term tail risk. Sub-15 VIX historically associated with continued positive equity momentum.

signals.label: Nasdaq / Tech
signals.note: AI infra theme strengthening into Q2 earnings — NVDA, META, MSFT all guiding above street. Small and mid-cap tech starting to catch up, suggesting sentiment broadening.

signals.label: Buffett Indicator
signals.note: Total US market cap ~185% of GDP — market breakout pushed ratio to new 2026 high. Fair value zone: 75–100%. Warrants disciplined position sizing even in conviction names.

signals.label: Shiller CAPE
signals.note: Near dot-com peak territory on a cyclically adjusted basis. The AI productivity premium is real but does not fully justify current levels relative to 10-year real earnings.

signals.label: S&P 500 Forward P/E
signals.note: Forward P/E expanding as price outpaces estimate revisions; positive for momentum but leaves limited cushion for any earnings miss or macro shock.

signals.label: S&P 500 Earnings Yield
signals.note: Equity risk premium slightly improved vs. May as 10Y Treasury fell to 4.18% — spread now ~47 bps positive vs. near-zero last month. Still the lowest risk premium since 2007.

signals.label: Fed Funds Rate
signals.note: FOMC held June 12 as expected. Updated dot plot: median now shows 1 cut in 2026, implying September. Markets pricing 72% probability of a September cut — the most hawkish credible path in 18 months.

signals.label: 10Y US Treasury
signals.note: Fell 17 bps from May peak as Iran framework deal drove oil lower and inflation expectations pulled back. A break below 4.0% would be a significant re-rating event for equities.

signals.label: Yield Curve (2s10s)
signals.note: Dis-inverting steadily — up from -83 bps peak. A positive 2s10s reading (historically achieved ~6–18 months before a recession ends) would be the strongest macro buy signal of this cycle.

signals.label: 10Y Real Yield
signals.note: Declining with nominal yields; still mildly restrictive but no longer compressing equity P/E multiples as aggressively as in March. A break below 1.5% would re-rate growth stocks significantly.

signals.label: 10Y Breakeven Inflation
signals.note: Falling rapidly — now the most credible disinflation signal since 2023. Oil pulling below $85 WTI is doing the heavy lifting. If this reaches 2.0%, the Fed cut becomes consensus.

signals.label: BoC Rate
signals.note: BoC held June 4. Canadian May CPI printed 2.1% — cleanest in G7. A July cut is now live base-case for many Bay Street economists. ZAG.TO positioned well for this scenario.

signals.label: US CPI (YoY)
signals.note: May CPI printed 3.0% — first sub-3.1% reading since October 2025; energy component falling fast. If June prints 2.7–2.8%, the September cut becomes near-certain.

signals.label: US Core PCE (YoY)
signals.note: April PCE came in at 2.65%; May estimate ~2.55%. Fed's 2% target still not achieved but trajectory is the clearest in 18 months. Services PCE easing but slowly.

signals.label: ISM Manufacturing
signals.note: Edging back toward the 50 expansion/contraction line; new orders sub-index showing green shoots. Not yet expansionary but no longer deteriorating.

signals.label: ISM Services
signals.note: Services sector reaccelerating modestly; business activity index strong. Consumer spending on services remains the bedrock of the soft-landing thesis.

signals.label: M2 Growth (YoY)
signals.note: M2 growth accelerating modestly — positive for risk assets but bears watching if it gets ahead of productivity gains. Not yet inflationary at this rate.

signals.label: US Unemployment
signals.note: May jobs report surprised to the upside: +185K payrolls, unemployment ticked down to 4.0%. Soft-landing scenario gaining credibility — labour market has not broken.

signals.label: Oil (WTI)
signals.note: Iran framework agreement (June 9) pushed WTI below $85 for the first time since February. A formal deal could see $70–75. This is the single biggest disinflationary catalyst of 2026.

signals.label: Gold
signals.note: Pulled back ~$45 from May highs as Iran optimism reduced safe-haven demand. Structural floor from central bank buying still intact — not a breakdown, just a rotation from fear to risk-on.

signals.label: USD/CAD
signals.note: CAD mildly weaker as oil retreats — partially offsetting the benefit of falling oil for Canadian consumers. BoC cut expectations also weighing on CAD vs USD.

signals.label: HY Credit Spread (CDX HY)
signals.note: Spreads compressing toward pre-conflict levels; HY market is effectively pricing a soft-landing scenario. A move below 320 bps would be all-clear for credit.

signals.label: IG Credit Spread
signals.note: Investment-grade at tightest spread since Jan 2026; corporate balance sheets broadly healthy, supporting dividend growers like ENB, CNQ, and Canadian banks.

signals.label: US HY Default Rate
signals.note: Rate of increase slowing — may be peaking if September cut materialises and refinancing stress eases. Still above 2025 trough of 2.4%; watch the next 60 days.

signals.label: Bank Lending Standards
signals.note: Net 16% of banks tightening C&I standards in Q2 survey, down from 22% in Q1 — pace of tightening is slowing. First sign that credit conditions may be bottoming.

signals.label: DXY (US Dollar Index)
signals.note: Dollar weakening on rate-cut pricing and oil-driven disinflation; DXY below 103 is a meaningful tailwind for NVDA, AMZN, and GOOGL international revenue. Also supportive of gold floor.

signals.label: Copper (front-month)
signals.note: New 9-month high — AI data center buildout and grid electrification are providing demand above and beyond the China construction cycle. Strong corroborating signal for tech capex.

signals.label: China PMI
signals.note: China manufacturing PMI back above 50 for the first time in 3 months; stimulus measures gaining traction. Positive for TSM (advanced packaging for Chinese AI customers) and commodity names.

signals.label: Eurozone CPI
signals.note: Eurozone hit the ECB's 2% target in May — ECB cut 25 bps at its June meeting. This is the global disinflationary tide that the Fed is watching; gives Powell political cover for September.

signals.label: Fear & Greed
signals.note: Crossed into Greed territory for first time since November 2025; Iran framework deal + FOMC dot-plot upgrade drove the shift. Approaching levels that historically signal near-term caution.

signals.label: AAII Bull/Bear
signals.note: Retail bullishness rising toward contrarian caution zone (>55%). Still not at Extreme Greed levels but watch for a spike that would signal crowded positioning.

signals.label: Put/Call Ratio
signals.note: Put hedging demand falling as VIX declines; below 0.8 historically signals complacency. Not a sell signal on its own but worth watching alongside rising equity prices.

signals.label: US Consumer Confidence
signals.note: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment jumped on falling gas prices; above 100 for the first time since Q3 2025. A significant tailwind for consumer discretionary names — BKNG, ABNB, UBER.

signals.label: Earnings Revisions
signals.note: FY2026 S&P 500 EPS estimates upgraded for the second consecutive month; AI-driven productivity gains are feeding through to margins. NVDA, META, MSFT led upward revisions.

newsSignals.headline: Iran Nuclear Framework Agreed; WTI Crude Falls Below $82 for First Time Since February
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: Oil below $85 is the single biggest near-term disinflationary catalyst — clears the path for a September Fed cut. Positive for rate-sensitive tech (NVDA, MSFT, NOW); mild headwind for CNQ near-term but dividend remains covered above $65 WTI.

newsSignals.headline: FOMC Holds at 3.50–3.75%; Updated Dot Plot Shows Median 1 Cut in 2026 — September Window Open
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: September cut now base case (72% market probability). Adds tailwind to growth stocks, particularly PLTR, NOW, and AMZN. Validates adding ZAG.TO in RRSP — duration should perform well into the cut. CAD mildly positive on risk-on.

newsSignals.headline: Apple Intelligence 2.0 Announced at WWDC: On-Device AI Across All Platforms, Siri Rebuilt on Foundation Model
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: Positive for AAPL (TFSA) — super-cycle upgrade thesis strengthened by AI feature set on iPhone 18 lineup due in September. Also supportive of AVGO (RRSP) custom AI silicon relationship with Apple.

newsSignals.headline: Microsoft Raises Full-Year Azure Guidance on AI Workload Surge; Capex Plan Tops $70B
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: Directly positive for MSFT (RRSP) and NVDA (TFSA) — $70B capex confirms H100/B200 demand runway well into 2027. Also lifts ANET and AMZN (AWS competing in same data centre buildout).

newsSignals.headline: Canada May CPI: 2.1% — Cleanest G7 Print; BoC July Cut Now Live Base-Case
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: BoC cut in July would steepen the GoC curve and re-rate Canadian banks (RY, TD, BMO hold NIMs better with a positive curve). ZAG.TO positioned for duration rally. Modest CAD headwind for USD-denominated holdings — partially offset by rising equity prices.

newsSignals.headline: Booking Holdings Reports Record Q1 International Travel Revenue; Raises FY2026 Guidance
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: BKNG now in scanner universe — strong Q1 and guidance upgrade puts it squarely in the GARP and Compounder screener buckets. Consumer confidence recovery (Michigan: 104) is a structural tailwind for the entire travel vertical: BKNG, ABNB, RCL, MAR.

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: Iran deal finalised; oil falls to $70–75; June CPI prints 2.7%; Fed cuts 25 bps Sept 17; 2s10s un-inverts — historically the strongest 12-month forward equity return signal of the past 40 years
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 36,000–37,000; BoC cuts July + September; ZAG.TO rallies 4–6%; CAD range-bound 1.37–1.40; Canadian banks re-rate on curve steepening
scenarios.positioning: Add NVDA on any 5% dips — B200 ramp is the 2026 earnings catalyst; deploy ZAG.TO in RRSP ahead of cut; add BKNG and ABNB on consumer confidence recovery; reduce GOLD to 4% (risk-on rotation)
scenarios.sectorRotation: Overweight Financials (steepening curve), Technology (rate cut re-rates growth), Consumer Discretionary (confidence recovery); underweight Utilities, Gold miners

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Iran framework holds but formal deal delayed to Q4; oil stays $78–88; Fed cuts once in September; AI earnings carry the index; 2s10s moves to -10 to +10 bps
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 33,500–35,500; BoC cuts in September; energy names range-bound on oil consolidation; SHOP strengthens on weaker CAD
scenarios.positioning: Hold NVDA, AMZN, PLTR — AI theme intact; DCA into VFV.TO and QQQM systematically; maintain GOLD at 5% weight as geopolitical hedge; add BKNG and RCL on any 5% pullback
scenarios.sectorRotation: Quality growth bias: mega-cap tech + consumer recovery; trim deep energy cyclicals on oil stability; mild rotation into Financials ahead of confirmed BoC cut

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Iran framework collapses; oil spikes back above $100; June CPI re-accelerates to 3.3%+; Fed forced to hold all year or hike; sentiment whiplash from 62 Fear & Greed to sub-30
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 30,000–32,000; BoC cuts cancelled; energy rallies briefly but equity risk-off dominates; CAD strengthens temporarily then reverses
scenarios.positioning: Rotate to GOLD (TFSA) and CNQ (TFSA); increase ZAG.TO short-duration in RRSP; trim PLTR and NOW; cut consumer discretionary (BKNG, ABNB) entirely — travel demand is rate-sensitive
scenarios.sectorRotation: Max defensives: Consumer Staples, Healthcare, Gold; avoid Financials (re-inversion crushes NIMs), Consumer Discretionary, Real Estate

keyEvents.event: US May CPI print — critical data for September cut probability
Q2 earnings season begins — AI capex and consumer travel are key readthroughs
BoC rate decision — July cut is live base-case given May CPI of 2.1%
FOMC — September cut window; dot plot and presser will be market-moving

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: 2 Fed cuts (Sept + Nov/Dec); 2s10s fully positive; AI cycle drives 15%+ EPS growth for S&P; Iran deal fully ratified; oil stable at $72–78; new Fed Chair seen as credible
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 37,000+; BoC 3 cuts total; housing stabilises; SHOP and Canadian financials lead; ZAG.TO rallies 6–9%; GoC positively sloped
scenarios.positioning: Increase equity allocation to 85%; full positions in NVDA, AMZN, AVGO, LLY; add BKNG and RCL for consumer cycle recovery; rotate ZAG.TO gains into VFV.TO
scenarios.sectorRotation: Overweight Financials (steeper curve major NIM tailwind), Technology, Consumer Discretionary; reduce Utilities (crowded defensives), Gold, Energy

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: 1–2 Fed cuts; curve normalising; AI cycle intact; growth moderates to 2.0–2.5%; Iran deal in limbo but not collapsing; 2s10s at 0 to +25 bps by year-end
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 34,000–36,500; BoC 2 cuts; ZAG.TO performs; Canadian banks grind higher on improving curve; CAD 1.37–1.42 range
scenarios.positioning: Stay the course with DCA; reinvest dividends systematically; maintain TFSA/RRSP placement discipline; 5% GOLD allocation; add BKNG and ABNB on dips for consumer recovery play
scenarios.sectorRotation: Barbell: quality growth (NVDA, MSFT) + consumer recovery (BKNG, ABNB, RCL) + defensives (KO, PG); reduce speculative small-cap

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Fed holds all year or hikes; stagflation entrenches; credit spreads widen to 500+ bps; earnings fall 8–12%; new Fed Chair loses credibility — bond market vigilantes return
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 27,000–30,000; housing correction sharpens; BoC emergency cuts; CAD weakens sharply; SHOP and consumer names hit hardest
scenarios.positioning: Raise cash to 20%; ZAG.TO to 20% of RRSP; hold GOLD at 8%; liquidate PLTR, MU, ARM leverage; reduce consumer discretionary entirely — BKNG and ABNB are high-beta in this scenario
scenarios.sectorRotation: Maximum defensives: Staples, Healthcare, Gold miners, short-duration bonds; exit all AI-momentum, crypto (BTCC), and consumer cyclicals

keyEvents.event: FOMC — first potential cut; market-defining event for H2 2026
Q3 earnings — consumer spending and AI capex are the two key themes to watch
US midterm elections — fiscal policy direction and Fed independence narrative
Year-end rebalancing + potential second Fed cut window

bullish.label: Iran deal finalised — oil to $70–75, June CPI sub-2.8%, Fed cuts September — the 3-step unlock scenario
2s10s yield curve un-inversion — historically the single strongest 12-month equity return signal; -40 bps now, on the cusp
AI capex super-cycle intact — MSFT $70B, AMZN $40B, GOOGL $75B capex plans locked in for 2026–27
Consumer confidence recovery (Michigan: 104) — structural tailwind for BKNG, ABNB, RCL, UBER consumer discretionary cycle
BoC July cut live — Canadian banks, ZAG.TO, and SHOP positioned to benefit from GoC curve steepening
Apple Intelligence 2.0 — iPhone 18 AI super-cycle in September could be the largest consumer electronics upgrade cycle in 5 years

bearish.label: Iran framework collapse — oil back above $100, CPI re-accelerates, Fed forced to hike, equity sentiment whiplash from Greed to Fear
September cut fails to materialise — market pricing 72% probability; a hold would trigger a violent repricing of growth multiples
Fed Chair confirmation debacle — any perception of political capture would trigger bond market vigilantes and a sharp curve steepening for wrong reasons
Consumer credit stress resurfaces — delinquencies at 10-year highs; if confidence 104 print reverses on job loss, consumer discretionary unwinds fast
Commercial real estate credit event — regional bank loan book stress not yet resolved; a catalyst could widen HY spreads 150+ bps rapidly
China PMI reversal — if China stimulus fades and PMI re-enters contraction, copper and TSM/ASML demand signals would reverse sharply

portfolioImplication.summary: The macro backdrop has improved materially since May. The Iran framework deal + FOMC dot-plot upgrade + falling oil have combined to open a credible September rate-cut window — the event markets have been waiting 18 months for. The yield curve's approach to un-inversion is the most important signal to monitor. Position for the cut while keeping quality-bias and size discipline: the Buffett Indicator at 185% means there is no margin for error in speculative positions. The key new theme is a consumer recovery trade — BKNG, ABNB, and RCL are now in the scanner universe and merit attention as confidence-driven compounders.

actions.action: Hold NVDA (TFSA) — B200 Blackwell ramp is the single largest earnings catalyst in the market; MSFT's $70B capex plan directly feeds NVDA revenue. Only trim above 20% of TFSA. Stop: $160.

Add AMZN on any 4–6% dips — AWS cloud revenue re-accelerating in Q2; $40B AI infra plan locked in. September rate cut re-rates the growth multiple further. Best large-cap risk/reward in the portfolio.
Add ZAG.TO in RRSP now — BoC July cut is live base-case with Canada CPI at 2.1%; duration will outperform as curve steepens. Ideal RRSP placement (no WHT, CAD-denominated, interest tax-deferred).

Watch BKNG — now in scanner universe (score: 70). Record Q1 travel revenue + consumer confidence at 104 is the setup. On any 5% dip toward scanner fair value, initiate a position in RRSP (no WHT drag on dividends, even though BKNG pays none).
Hold GOLD (TFSA) at 5% — pulled back $45 from May highs on Iran optimism; maintain the position as geopolitical floor from central bank buying is intact. Only trim if Iran deal is formally ratified.
Hold CNQ (TFSA) — oil at $82 still covers the 4.5% dividend comfortably; exit thesis triggers below $65 WTI. Near-term headwind from Iran deal but the long-run oil sands cost advantage is unchanged.
Watch ABNB — scanner score 81, fwdPEG 0.71. Consumer confidence recovery trade with near-zero debt. A 5–7% pullback would be an attractive entry; allocate in TFSA for tax-free appreciation.
Add AVGO (RRSP) on any pullback — custom ASIC demand from Apple, MSFT, and Meta is doubling; dividend grows 15%+ annually. RRSP placement eliminates 0.7% WHT drag. Target weight: 5% of RRSP.

Watch RCL — scanner score 72, fwdPEG 0.53 (cheapest new addition on growth). Consumer travel recovery + lower oil (fuel cost tailwind) is the bull case. High D/E (2.5×) makes it rate-sensitive — buy only after September cut confirmed.
Hold BTCC (TFSA) at 5% cap — risk appetite improving but crypto remains the highest-beta position. Don't add; consider trimming if it exceeds 5% weight on any crypto rally.
